title: Bledsoe's disease
content:
{{Top|leg}}

|createdby=[[Kaaldar]] (Kaaldar's strain)<ref name="Tatooine Sojourn">"[[Tatooine Sojourn]]"</ref>
|dateengineered=c. [[0 ABY/Legends|0 ABY]] (Kaaldar's strain)<ref name="Date">The events of ''[[Tatooine Sojourn]]'' implied that it took place after the Rebel Alliance's [[Battle of Yavin/Legends|destruction of the Death Star]] at Yavin, which takes place in [[0 ABY/Legends|0 ABY]], according to ''[[The New Essential Chronology]]''.</ref>
|numberinfected=

{{Quote|Bledsoe's is contagious. It must not be allowed to spread... to us!|[[Doctor/Legends|Dr.]] [[Kaaldar]]|<ref name="Tatooine Sojourn" />}}
'''Bledsoe's disease''' was a virus transmitted by air and physical contact.<ref name="Encyclopedia">''[[The Complete Star Wars Encyclopedia]]''</ref> [[Squill (creature)|Squills]] were known to be carriers of the disease.<ref name="Tatooine Sojourn" />

Different strains of the disease existed, each causing unique reactions. Commonly, Bledsoe's caused the skin to burn, followed gradually by discoloration of the [[Eye/Legends|eyes]]. The discoloration resolved itself into a [[Star/Legends|starfield]] pattern, which, despite appearances, had absolutely no impact on the eyesight of the afflicted. Late stages of the disease also include a sense of dizziness.<ref name="Tatooine Sojourn" />

===Kaaldar's secret strain and the Imperial plot===
[[File:BledsoeSerum.jpg|thumb|left|250px|[[Kaaldar]] injecting [[Luke Skywalker/Legends|Luke Skywalker]] with [[Serum (Bledsoe's disease)|the serum]] for his strain of Bledsoe's as [[Anduvil]] looks on]]

Around [[0 ABY/Legends|0 ABY]], a strain of the virus was created by Dr [[Kaaldar]], an elite [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Imperial]] [[Scientist/Legends|scientist]], and an outbreak was orchestrated. The purpose was twofold: to undermine the social order of dissatisfied worlds, and to locate secret bases of the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel Alliance]]. Genetic manipulation of the virus could encode information that would affect the symptoms; one symptom of the disease, referred to as the "window effect," was thus engineered as a secret courier of star charts. Through the eyes of afflicted individuals, Imperial scientists could locate a star map via an [[ocular printer]] which revealed the location of Rebel bases for targeting by the [[Imperial Navy/Legends|Imperial Fleet]].<ref name="Tatooine Sojourn" /><ref name="Encyclopedia" />

Imperial forces watched for outbreaks of Bledsoe's and used the discoloration as a silent means of communication and strategics. However, the effect did not last long after [[Death/Legends|death]], as evidenced by various [[Stormtrooper/Legends|stormtroopers]] being unable to find anything in the eyes of [[Jawa/Legends|Jawas]] that were long deceased upon finding their [[Sandcrawler/Legends|sandcrawler]].

As a means of ensuring this, the Imperials injected several [[Squill (creature)|squills]] with the Bledsoe's disease. Squills were naturally resistant to the disease, thus making them excellent carriers. The squills were then placed into transport containers and launched towards unsuspecting planets suspected of harboring Rebel sympathizers, including [[Tay'ah'loo]], [[Ogem/Legends|Ogem]], and Tatooine. Although the infection of the former two planets went as planned, with the Rebel Outpost on Kholes being located and wiped out by the Empire shortly thereafter, the latter planet backfired after a troupe of Jawas unwittingly intercepted the transport capsule. In addition, it was frequent enough that the Rebel Alliance eventually ordered [[Luke Skywalker/Legends|Luke Skywalker]] to carry out a [[Mission to Tatooine (Luke Skywalker)|mission]] to Tatooine to investigate, to the latter's reluctance.

==Behind the scenes==
[[File:BledsoesDisease-TatooineSojournBW.png|thumb|150px|Star in the eyes, as it appeared in the 1979 original version of the strip (note the ringed planetary object at the bottom right portion of the eye).]]
There was a slight change to the "stars in the eyes" effect between ''[[Tatooine Sojourn]]''{{'s}} initial release in [[1979]] and the republished [[Color/Legends|color]] version. In the original release, after Luke Skywalker was infected with Bledsoe's, a ringed [[Planet/Legends|planetary]] body was in the bottom right portion of his "star eye." This planetary body was removed in the rereleased colored versions.
